chunk_1854.text:
FAIR VALUE GAP ANALYSIS
Good afternoon, folks. Happy Saturday to you. We're going to do a little bit of a review today. Here's the Dollar Index on our weekly chart. There is a Fair Value Gap (FVG) that has been left in place from last week's trading, which is this weekly candle. I want to see if we can keep this level in the form of resistance. In other words, price can go up here, hit it, and how we trade after we go into it is going to be If we were to go above the FVG, come back down, I would expect it to act as support. Maybe reach up into the rejection block, which is this highest up-close price here. Eventually target the high and the consequent encroachment of that wick.

chunk_4231.text:
DOLLAR INDEX (DXY) WEEKLY CHART ANALYSIS
But let's get right into it here's the Dollar Index, is the weekly chart and I'm watching this candle right here. Now usually I'll have a highlighted cursor but you're going to have to work with me here tonight, okay? We're doing, uh, sounds like my dogs are tearing the place up upstairs. Bearish order block, everything in this video is rough cut, okay? So these are things that I would usually edit out, but we're going to have to work through it, won't we? So this up-closed candle here that opening price, I'm extending that to the right. Do that on your weekly chart and you'll have the level I believe is likely to see Dollar Index draw up into. We've had this imbalance here and we've passed through it twice and I'm thinking it might lend support now, so it's inversion fair value gap. That's how I'm treating it. And you'll see this orange shaded area transposed in the lower time frame charts as we go through. On a discount, I'm looking at these two levels here. We have the weekly low of this current week and then two weeks ago that high. So that's a discount. So I'm anticipating and expecting price to want to gravitate towards this candle's opening price which is the bearish order block on the weekly chart, okay? So don't get lost in the dialogue without making sure you understand what chart I'm showing up here in the upper left-hand corner. It's a weekly chart, so these levels will transpose when we go into the daily chart.
chunk_4231.source: Tuesday May 23, 2023 Forex & Spooz Market Review_Handbook.blocks.json

chunk_2627.text:
DOLLAR INDEX SEASONAL TENDENCY AND KEY MOVEMENTS
If we have the Dollar Index as a seasonal tendency, should this be occurring at the same time that we see the June-July rally? Should that be occurring with a bullish or bearish move in the Dollar Index? Here's that seasonal tendency for the Dollar, and you can see around the January-February time there's usually a rally that takes place, which is contrary to what we saw in the 10-year treasury notes declining. Then we have a significant high forming between June and July, and then the Dollar Index typically trades down the rest of the year, usually making a low around the last week of October, first week of November, and it creates a small little bounce on November time period for the Dollar Index.
chunk_2627.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 05 - Using 10 Year Notes In HTF Analysis_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_2627.section_title: DOLLAR INDEX SEASONAL TENDENCY AND KEY MOVEMENTS

chunk_2101.text:
DXY DAILY CHART
Transitioning to the daily chart for the Dollar Index: Price has again worked up into the weekly mean threshold of the bearish order block. It's crucial to transpose higher time frame PD (Price Delivery) arrays onto lower time frames, as a weekly fair value gap may not appear as such on the daily chart. A daily fair value gap is identified where price previously traded into. A down-close candle is being watched for potential order block behavior. Price might return into this area. A breaker pattern (high, low, higher high) is identified, suggesting price may retest this area. The entire down-closed candle represents a bearish breaker. If price accelerates below this low, the liquidity present should be a likely longer-term draw.

chunk_1785.text:
DOLLAR INDEX (DXY) DAILY CHART ANALYSIS
On the daily chart, we got real close to the target but didn't quite reach it. Now, price has pulled up into this inefficiency. There's a small gap, which is actually a real liquidity void where no actual trading was printed between these two respective candles (this candle's high, next candle's low). It will be interesting to see if it wants to reprice into this little gap. If it stays open and starts to trade lower, this can be something that can stay open for a long time.

The market 'keyed off' the 4134.25 level (an old New Week Opening Gap low) but 'failed to go to the buy side.' It then respected the 'consequent encroachment' of the teal-colored 5-minute Fair Value Gap, leading to a move lower. However, it 'failed to touch' a specific FVG, which would have signaled a further lower low. Crucially, the Dollar Index is still not moving to a higher high, maintaining a critical divergence.

DXY DAILY

chunk_655.text:
DOLLAR INDEX HISTORICAL MOVEMENT AND CURRENT EXPECTATIONS
We've observed a movement from the highs in October 2022, down to the sell side. We previously anticipated a move into a fair value gap (buyside imbalance, sell-side inefficiency) and an order block. If we had gone below a certain low, consequent encroachment (midpoint of the gap) was a potential target. The market structure shifted bullish, leading to a bullish breaker and subsequent consolidation around a higher order block. Price then rallied into a volume imbalance, traded down into it, and worked its way higher. We've been live, real-time, discussing these movements. My attention is now drawn to an area up ahead, and we are currently marching directly into it.
chunk_655.section_title: DOLLAR INDEX HISTORICAL MOVEMENT AND CURRENT EXPECTATIONS

chunk_3.text:
FAIR VALUE GAP DYNAMICS
A Fair Value Gap (FVG) was observed on the 15-minute timeframe. After running the buy side, the market drew down, took out sell side, and then overran this FVG, trading into a larger FVG. The rationale for this behavior is that if the market intends to trade above a smaller FVG (reaching the low end of a specific candle), it is likely to continue into a larger, more prominent FVG.

chunk_600.text:
INITIAL CPI PRICE ACTION AND DXY INFLUENCE
I'm trying to be very careful how I talk to you today because some of you just really want to push a button. So we've taken Buy side out now after reaching down into that old 15-minute Fair Value Gap. Dollar Index, if you look at the low one dollar for February 9th, we are just now piercing that low. So I'm watching, do we get any kind of rejection there on the dollar, then start to move higher? Then this is suggesting that maybe that Buy side liquidity pool for ES was just a raid. And I don't want to make any kind of determination while I'm talking to you here because I don't want to put anybody in a painful situation. Just it's still unfolding with the CPI number. Dollar Index is coming off of its February 9th low. I'm not saying it's reversed, but I'm just watching that right now. You can pull that up on TradingView with the symbol DX, it's free to see it.
chunk_600.source: February 14, 2023 ES Live Commentary CPI Release_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_600.section_title: INITIAL CPI PRICE ACTION AND DXY INFLUENCE

chunk_4172.text:
FRAMING MARKET LOGIC WITH DXY
All my analysis, across Forex, bond markets, equities, and index futures, starts with the Dollar Index. It frames the logic for whether I should assume risk (e.g., buying foreign currencies or stocks), which correlates with a lower dollar (risk-on). At the beginning of the year, we were indeed looking for a lower dollar, as documented in my YouTube analysis and Twitter commentary. Our prior analysis for DXY looked for lower prices and sell-side liquidity, which was absolutely tagged. If price went below that, we identified an order block and a fair value gap. This fair value gap is a buy-side imbalance sell-side inefficiency, occurring between a candle's high and a subsequent candle's low.

chunk_629.text:
ES FVG BREAK AND MARKET UNCERTAINTY
ES has broken through its Fair Value Gap (FVG), and the Dollar Index appears to be expanding higher, making a retracement lower on DXY less likely. This suggests a potential drop off the FVG on ES. The market currently exhibits 'a whole lot of uncertainty back and forth,' which is precisely what is being observed. The key takeaway in such an environment is whether one wants to trade in these conditions.
chunk_629.source: February 15, 2023 ES Live Commentary AM Opening Session_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_629.section_title: ES FVG BREAK AND MARKET UNCERTAINTY

chunk_345.text:
IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS (FVG) AFTER DISPLACEMENT
After a swing high is taken out and an energetic break below it occurs: Look for a clear Fair Value Gap (FVG) formed by a smashing down movement. The low, the high, and if price trades back up into that FVG, you look for a short entry. Target the sell-side liquidity resting below the swing low identified prior to 8:30 AM.
chunk_345.section_title: IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS (FVG) AFTER DISPLACEMENT

chunk_429.text:
FAIR VALUE GAP AS DYNAMIC SUPPORT
The five-minute Fair Value Gap is now above the current price level. In a correct bullish narrative, when price is above a Fair Value Gap, it tends to act as support. This behavior is contrasted with classic support and resistance, which 'hardly ever works'. The FVG is the expected mechanism for support in this scenario.
chunk_429.source: 5 Handle Twitter Example Live Execution... January 30, 2023_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_429.section_title: FAIR VALUE GAP AS DYNAMIC SUPPORT

MARKET CONTEXT AND INTERMARKET DIVERGENCE
The market gapped up, had a small rally, then dropped, but not aggressively. Intermarket analysis with the dollar cancelled any short idea at that point. The pop to 4028 indicates a run on buy stops, killing anyone trying to short the initial gap higher. Watching DXY's original Fair Value Gap (9:44 1-min candle) to see if it rallies. If DXY rallies, the current high on ES (4028) is 'suspect,' indicating it's just a run on buy stops before a Price must confirm; this is anticipation of likelihood.

chunk_214.text:
IDENTIFYING THE FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG) AND ENTRY
Again, don't take my word for it; you're going to be flabbergasted when you see how many times this thing forms every single week. It's many times throughout the intraday charts it creates this type of move, but it runs the stops, then we have a short-term low, and then it breaks below it. So now we have a break in Market structure. Once this low is broken, you're going to look for this little area here that's that imbalance I mentioned in the beginning, right? So, what's happening is the market's going to go right up inside that Now, if you don't sell there, you can drop down to a lower time frame, a one-minute chart. If this was a three-minute chart, you can go down to a one-minute chart and look for that to occur on that time frame as well, and it many times will form. If you're looking at a lower time frame, like say this was a five-minute chart and you looked at a one-minute chart, you'd find one down in here. It's a matter of scaling down in your time frames, because once you have an underlying premise to the market now likely to go lower, it becomes an easy thing to look for these types of things.
chunk_214.section_title: IDENTIFYING THE FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG) AND ENTRY

chunk_1348.text:
DXY 15-MINUTE ANALYSIS
On the 15-minute time frame, buy-side liquidity here and sell-side liquidity here during the London session. The midnight opening candle had its opening price extended through. If bearish, looking for lower prices, meaning it is a risk-on scenario. If the dollar rallies, it will take out buy-side or trade into an inefficiency in the form of SIBI or fair value gap.

chunk_471.text:
DETAILED EXPLANATION OF FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG)
A detailed explanation of the Fair Value Gap is provided, highlighting the mechanics of efficient versus Price started a rally, came up to a candle's high, and then repriced back down into a low. This portion is The concept is explained using a paint roller analogy: initially, paint is amply distributed, but as the roller moves higher, it runs out of paint, creating 'porous pockets' or areas of unequal distribution. The FVG is the area where paint (price) wasn't equally distributed, specifically a gap between one candle's high and the next candle after a large up-close candle, where nothing was offered back down to touch the prior candle's high. This signifies a lack of symmetric price delivery.
chunk_471.source: December 2023 Non Farm Payroll Live Execution & Explanations_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_471.section_title: DETAILED EXPLANATION OF FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG)

DOLLAR INDEX DIVERGENCE AND ES BUY-SIDE POTENTIAL
ES moved to the sell-side without the Dollar Index (DXY) confirming with a 'higher high.' This divergence is NASDAQ Targets: For NASDAQ, key levels are 12540 and the morning low at 12534.25. DXY Confirmation Needed: DXY needs to show significant strength for conviction in ES's lower run. Potential for Accumulation: Without DXY making a higher high, the ES run lower might be 'smart money' accumulating longs at the sell-side, intending to drive price to the buy-side at 4136.50. This is considered a potential setup in the current choppy, aimless market. For this scenario to hold, ES would need to remain below the consequent encroachment of the teal-colored FVG at 4128.

MARKET REVISITATION OF FAIR VALUE GAPS
The market's tendency to revisit Fair Value Gaps is explained. Price bumped into the identified SIBI on Friday, with the candle's high at 4547.75, just a quarter of a point After touching this 'premium' area of the FVG, price retraced lower. This observation is part of early pre-market analysis, before the market officially opens, noting that while it touched the premium, it doesn't preclude further upward movement.

chunk_3513.text:
THE US DOLLAR INDEX AS A FOUNDATION
Every significant move in the Forex or currency markets has its roots in the price action of the US Dollar Index. A significant price move in a currency will be mirrored in the DXY. While the Dollar Index can consolidate on very short-term or intraday charts, and foreign currencies can still trade stronger or weaker against it, the search for Mega Trades fundamentally depends on the Dollar Index supporting the trade idea. Without DXY support, it is not a qualified Mega Trade setup.
chunk_3513.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 11 - Forex & Currency Mega-Trades_Handbook.blocks.json
chunk_3513.section_title: THE US DOLLAR INDEX AS A FOUNDATION

chunk_3876.text:
INTERMARKET DIVERGENCE AND SHORT-TERM LOWS
Dollar Index making a higher high here. So we would want to see that low taken out on ES, that hasn't happened yet, but dollar has made a higher high. So if this doesn't make the lower low, we might be seeing a short-term low forming in ES and trade up a little bit. Dollar is coming off the highs. And ES can attempt to reach into a little bit deeper in this Gap right here. I'm looking at the dollar index in a five minute chart and you scroll into a 15-minute chart in an hourly chart. And I'm monitoring, does it continuously provide to you that it wants to keep going higher? So far it hasn't made a higher high than the high it's already produced, which is problematic for ES continuing at least near term on the downside. So we're just watching and see what we get in terms of delivery on ES. Also, if you look at the Dow Jones, Dow futures, YMH2023, it's made a lower low where ES has not done so either. So whenever we're looking at that, this gives reason to be concerned about either consolidation or a potential short-term reversal.
chunk_3876.source: March 07, 2023 Live Tape Reading ⧹ Fed Chair Testimony 10am_Handbook.blocks.json

DOLLAR INDEX AND FOREIGN CURRENCY DAILY BIAS
If we're doing that analysis on a daily for the dollar index and we've arrived at a bullishness for the dollar, what does that translate for foreign currency in terms of daily bias? It's going to be a mirror image. So if we go to the Euro Dollar, we can see that the Euro Dollar has been bearish as we would reasonably expect.

chunk_2362.text:
TIMING AND FAIR VALUE GAPS
Gaps like these usually occur in the New York session, typically around 8:30 AM (when news embargoes lift), allowing more time for the market to unfold. If a gap occurs later, such as the 10 or 11 o'clock hour in New York, a portion of the gap may remain open. This remaining open portion would be classified as a fair value gap, to be potentially closed at a later time, but not necessarily during the specific trading day.
chunk_2362.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 04 - ICT Vacuum Block_Handbook.blocks.json
